news.titleEn: Brent oil jumps more than 9%, biggest daily gain since 2020, after Trump reinstates Iran blockade
news.conclusionEn: Brent crude surged 9.6% to close at $83.30 a barrel, its best daily performance since 2020, after Iran struck two UAE-owned supertankers in the Strait of Hormuz and Trump announced a renewed US naval blockade of Iran.
news.conclusionZh: 布伦特原油单日暴涨9.6%，收于每桶83.30美元，为2020年以来最大单日涨幅，此前伊朗在霍尔木兹海峡袭击两艘阿联酋籍超级油轮，特朗普宣布美国重启对伊朗的海上封锁。
news.whyEn: The Strait of Hormuz carries about a fifth of global oil shipments, so a confirmed tanker attack plus a US blockade announcement removes any doubt that supply risk is real, not just rhetoric — driving fuel and shipping costs higher worldwide just as central banks assess inflation trends.
news.whyZh: 霍尔木兹海峡承担全球约五分之一的石油运输，油轮遇袭加上美国宣布封锁，证实供应风险已成现实而非空谈，推高全球燃料与运输成本，恰逢各国央行评估通胀走势的关键时刻。
news.detailEn: Iran struck the tankers Mombasa B and Al Bahyah with cruise missiles on Tuesday, killing one sailor and injuring eight others, according to UAE officials. Trump responded by saying the US would reimpose a naval blockade against Iran and called for Gulf states benefiting from the protection — Saudi Arabia, the UAE, Qatar, Bahrain and Kuwait — to reimburse Washington 20% of the value of secured cargoes. The escalation shatters what remained of the interim ceasefire signed in mid-June, and traders are now pricing in the possibility that tanker traffic through the strait could be disrupted for an extended period.
news.detailZh: 据阿联酋官员称，伊朗周二用巡航导弹袭击了「蒙巴萨B号」和「巴哈亚号」两艘油轮，造成1名船员死亡、8人受伤。特朗普随即表示，美国将重新对伊朗实施海上封锁，并要求受此保护的海湾国家——沙特、阿联酋、卡塔尔、巴林和科威特——向美方支付所护送货物价值20%的费用作为补偿。此次升级使6月中旬签署的临时停火协议名存实亡，交易员目前已将海峡航运可能长期中断的风险计入油价。

news.titleEn: US Inflation: CPI Falls for First Time Since 2020, Core Gauge Unchanged
news.conclusionEn: US consumer prices fell 0.4% in June, the first monthly drop since 2020, pulling annual inflation down to 3.5% from May's 4.2% as gasoline prices tumbled 9.7%.
news.conclusionZh: 美国6月CPI环比下降0.4%，为2020年以来首次月度下降，年通胀率从5月的4.2%降至3.5%，汽油价格暴跌9.7%。
news.whyEn: The much cooler-than-expected inflation reading eases pressure on the Fed to hike rates and gives Chair Kevin Warsh more room during his debut congressional testimony this week, directly shaping expectations for stocks, bonds and the dollar.
news.whyZh: 远低于预期的通胀数据缓解了美联储加息的压力，也为主席沃什本周首次国会作证提供了更大空间，直接影响股市、债市和美元的预期走向。
news.detailEn: Economists had forecast only a 0.2% monthly decline and a 3.8% annual rate, so the actual figures came in well below consensus. The drop was driven almost entirely by a sharp pullback in energy prices, with the broader energy index falling 5.7% after rising 3.9% in May, alongside easing housing and services costs. Core inflation, which strips out food and energy, was flat for the month versus an expected 0.2% rise, pushing the 12-month core rate down to 2.6%. Economists cautioned the relief could be temporary given the fresh oil price spike from the renewed Iran conflict, which was not yet fully reflected in June's data.
news.detailZh: 经济学家此前预测CPI环比仅下降0.2%、年率为3.8%，实际数据大幅低于市场共识。此次下降几乎完全由能源价格大幅回落驱动，广义能源指数下跌5.7%（此前5月上涨3.9%），住房和服务成本也有所缓和。核心通胀（剔除食品和能源）环比持平，低于预期的0.2%涨幅，12个月核心通胀率降至2.6%。经济学家提醒，鉴于伊朗冲突重燃导致的新一轮油价飙升尚未完全反映在6月数据中，这种缓解可能只是暂时的。

news.titleEn: TSMC, ASML Face Earnings Scrutiny After $1.7 Trillion Chip Rout
news.conclusionEn: ASML reports earnings July 15 and TSMC follows July 16, with investors watching capex and capacity guidance closely after a chip-sector selloff erased roughly $1.7 trillion in market value since late June.
news.conclusionZh: ASML将于7月15日、台积电将于7月16日发布财报，此前芯片板块抛售潮已抹去约1.7万亿美元市值，投资者将密切关注两家公司的资本支出与产能指引。
news.whyEn: As the two most important suppliers underpinning the global AI chip supply chain, their results and forward guidance will offer the clearest signal yet on whether the AI infrastructure buildout still has momentum or is starting to peak, directly moving sentiment across the entire tech sector.
news.whyZh: 作为支撑全球AI芯片供应链的两大核心供应商，两家公司的业绩与前瞻指引将成为判断AI基建投资热潮是否仍有动能、抑或已现顶峰的最清晰信号，直接左右整个科技板块情绪。
news.detailEn: TSMC's revenue for the first half of 2026 hit NT$2.404 trillion, up 35.6% year-over-year, and the company has guided for more than 30% full-year dollar revenue growth on the back of AI-related chip demand. Capital expenditure figures from TSMC and ASML's capacity roadmap will be the most closely watched numbers, since they signal how much further semiconductor equipment makers and foundries expect the AI buildout to run. The results come at a moment of acute investor unease after memory and semiconductor stocks fell sharply from their June highs on fears that AI infrastructure spending has outrun near-term demand.
news.detailZh: 台积电2026年上半年营收达2.404万亿新台币，同比增长35.6%，公司此前预计全年美元营收增速将超过30%，主要受AI相关芯片需求驱动。台积电的资本支出数据及ASML的产能规划将是市场最关注的指标，因为它们将揭示半导体设备商与代工厂对AI基建热潮持续性的预期。此次财报发布正值投资者情绪高度紧张之际——此前内存和半导体股票因担忧AI基础设施支出已超出近期实际需求，从6月高点大幅回落。

news.titleEn: The AI boom just found two new winners: Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan Chase
news.conclusionEn: Goldman Sachs posted Q2 earnings of $20.98 per share versus $14.48 expected, and JPMorgan's core profit hit $6.14 per share versus $5.80 expected, as both banks logged record trading revenue fueled by AI-driven market activity.
news.conclusionZh: 高盛第二季度每股盈利20.98美元，远超预期的14.48美元；摩根大通核心每股盈利6.14美元，高于预期的5.80美元，两家银行的交易收入均创纪录，受AI驱动的市场活跃度提振。
news.whyEn: Wall Street's biggest banks posting record trading results shows AI-related volatility and dealmaking are becoming a direct profit driver for financial firms, not just tech companies, reinforcing confidence in this earnings season even amid geopolitical turmoil.
news.whyZh: 华尔街大行交易业务创纪录，显示AI相关的市场波动与并购活动正成为金融机构（而不仅是科技公司）的直接利润来源，即便地缘政治动荡不断，本轮财报季信心依然稳固。
news.detailEn: Goldman's revenue surged to $20.34 billion from $14.58 billion a year earlier, with shares jumping 9% as the bank posted its best-ever trading quarter. JPMorgan reported net income of $21.2 billion, including a $4.6 billion one-time gain on its Visa stake, with core operating profit still hitting a record $16.9 billion. Both banks said AI-driven volatility, algorithmic trading volumes, and a resurgence in dealmaking activity — including large tech IPOs — propelled results well above what was already an optimistic consensus heading into the quarter.
news.detailZh: 高盛营收从去年同期的145.8亿美元跃升至203.4亿美元，股价当日上涨9%，创下该行有史以来最佳交易业务季度。摩根大通净利润为212亿美元，其中包含46亿美元来自Visa股权的一次性收益，剔除后核心营业利润仍创下169亿美元的纪录。两家银行均表示，AI驱动的市场波动、算法交易量增长以及并购活动（包括大型科技公司IPO）的回暖，共同推动业绩大幅超越本已乐观的市场预期。

news.titleEn: IBM stock craters 25%, the worst day on record, after company issues second-quarter earnings warning
news.conclusionEn: IBM shares plunged 25% — its worst single-day drop on record, surpassing 1987's Black Monday decline — after the company preliminarily reported Q2 revenue of $17.2 billion and adjusted EPS of $2.93, both below expectations.
news.conclusionZh: IBM股价暴跌25%，创该公司有史以来最大单日跌幅（超过1987年「黑色星期一」），此前公司初步公布第二季度营收172亿美元、调整后每股盈利2.93美元，均低于市场预期。
news.whyEn: A legacy tech giant warning that clients are diverting budget toward AI infrastructure like memory chips and servers, at the expense of software spending, signals a broader shift in corporate IT budgets that could pressure other enterprise software companies.
news.whyZh: 这家老牌科技巨头警告称，客户正将预算从软件转向内存芯片、服务器等AI基础设施，这一信号预示企业IT预算的更广泛转移趋势，可能给其他企业软件公司带来压力。
news.detailEn: CEO Arvind Krishna attributed the shortfall mainly to weakness in IBM's software and infrastructure businesses, as clients redirected quarterly capital spending toward servers, storage and memory purchases to secure supply-constrained hardware ahead of expected price increases. Several large deals also failed to close within the expected timeline. Analysts said the warning raises fresh concern that AI-driven infrastructure demand is disrupting established software vendors' business models even as it boosts hardware and chip makers.
news.detailZh: 首席执行官阿尔文德·克里希纳表示，业绩不及预期主要归因于软件与基础设施业务疲软，因为客户将季度资本支出转向服务器、存储和内存芯片采购，以在预期涨价前锁定供应紧张的硬件。此外多笔大型交易未能按预期时间完成。分析师指出，此次预警加剧了市场对AI驱动的基础设施需求正在冲击传统软件厂商商业模式的担忧，即便这一趋势正提振硬件和芯片制造商的业绩。

news.titleEn: Trump administration vows to 'disable' International Criminal Court
news.conclusionEn: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio announced a sweeping 'whole-of-government' campaign to systematically disable the ICC, including sanctions, travel bans, and pressure on other nations to withdraw support and funding.
news.conclusionZh: 美国国务卿鲁比奥宣布发起「全政府」行动，系统性削弱国际刑事法院，包括制裁、旅行禁令，并向其他国家施压要求撤回支持与资金。
news.whyEn: A major power moving to systematically dismantle an international judicial institution reshapes the landscape of international law enforcement and could trigger diplomatic friction with allied nations that continue to support the court.
news.whyZh: 一个主要大国系统性打压国际司法机构，将重塑国际执法格局，也可能与继续支持该法院的盟国产生外交摩擦。
news.detailEn: The State Department said the ICC poses 'an intolerable threat' to US sovereignty because it claims authority to prosecute American servicemen and officials, and it vowed to pressure other countries to withdraw from the court and cut off financial support. The move follows the Trump administration's earlier sanctions against several ICC judges over investigations into US personnel, which those judges are now challenging in a lawsuit filed in June. Amnesty International and other rights groups have condemned the campaign, calling on states to resist US pressure and continue supporting the court's mandate.
news.detailZh: 美国国务院表示，国际刑事法院对美国主权构成「不可容忍的威胁」，因其声称拥有起诉美国军人和官员的管辖权，并誓言向其他国家施压，要求其退出该法院并切断资金支持。此举延续了特朗普政府此前对多名国际刑事法院法官的制裁（因其调查美方人员），这些法官已于6月提起诉讼反制。国际特赦组织等人权团体谴责了这一行动，呼吁各国抵制美国的压力，继续支持该法院履行职责。

news.titleEn: Death toll from Bangkok bar fire rises to 32 as 2 more die in hospital
news.conclusionEn: The death toll from Sunday night's fire at the Rong Beer Na Ladprao music bar in Bangkok rose to 32 after two more injured victims died in hospital, with more than 70 people injured and 24 still in critical condition.
news.conclusionZh: 曼谷Rong Beer Na Ladprao音乐酒吧周日夜间火灾死亡人数升至32人，另有两名伤者在医院不治，超70人受伤，24人仍处于危重状态。
news.whyEn: The rising toll from one of Thailand's deadliest nightlife venue fires is intensifying scrutiny of fire-safety enforcement at entertainment venues in a country that depends heavily on tourism revenue.
news.whyZh: 死亡人数持续攀升，成为泰国娱乐场所史上最致命火灾之一，促使外界加强审视这个高度依赖旅游收入的国家在娱乐场所消防安全执法上的漏洞。
news.detailEn: Most of the victims were found trapped in windowless bathrooms at the back of the venue, where police believe they had fled in an attempt to escape the smoke and flames. Thai authorities say the cause of the fire, which broke out late Sunday night, remains under investigation, with earlier reports pointing to a circuit breaker malfunction near the stage. Dozens of the injured remain hospitalized, and the Bangkok Metropolitan Administration said the death toll could still rise further as some patients remain in critical condition.
news.detailZh: 多数遇难者是在场馆后方没有窗户的洗手间内被发现的，警方认为他们当时试图借此躲避浓烟和火势。泰国当局表示，这场周日深夜发生的火灾起因仍在调查中，早前报道指向舞台附近的断路器故障。数十名伤者仍在住院治疗，曼谷市政府表示，由于部分患者仍处于危重状态，死亡人数可能进一步上升。

businessTips.explanationEn: Second-order thinking means asking not just "what happens next" but "and then what happens after that" before making a decision — considering the consequences of the consequences, not just the immediate, obvious effect. Most people stop at first-order thinking, which looks appealing in isolation but can trigger reactions, side effects, or feedback loops that undo the initial benefit. Skilled strategists and operators train themselves to map out at least two or three moves ahead before committing to a plan.
businessTips.explanationZh: 二阶思维是指做决策前不仅问「接下来会发生什么」，还要追问「那之后又会发生什么」——考虑后果的后果，而不仅是最直接、最显而易见的影响。多数人只停留在一阶思维，这类决策单独看似不错，却可能引发连锁反应、副作用或反馈循环，最终抵消最初的好处。优秀的战略家和管理者会训练自己在做出决定前至少推演两到三步。
businessTips.exampleEn: A company cuts prices to win market share — the first-order effect looks great, more customers sign up. But competitors match the cut, margins across the industry collapse, and the company ends up with the same market share but far less profit, the second-order effect that undoes the initial win.
businessTips.exampleZh: 一家公司降价以争夺市场份额——一阶效果看起来不错，客户明显增多。但竞争对手随即跟进降价，全行业利润率崩溃，最终该公司市场份额没变，利润却大幅缩水，这就是抵消了最初收益的二阶效果。

businessTips.explanationEn: A productized service packages a consulting or freelance offering into a fixed scope, fixed price, and fixed timeline — like a product — instead of selling open-ended hourly work. This makes the offer easier for customers to understand and buy, lets the founder systemize delivery and eventually delegate it, and removes the income ceiling that comes from trading hours for dollars. It's a common way for consultants and side-hustlers to scale into a real business without hiring a large team.
businessTips.explanationZh: 产品化服务是把咨询或自由职业的服务打包成固定范围、固定价格、固定周期的「产品」，而不是按小时收费的无限期服务。这让客户更容易理解和购买，也让创始人能把交付流程标准化、日后外包给他人执行，从而摆脱「拿时间换钱」的收入天花板。这是许多顾问和副业者把业务发展成真正生意的常见路径，且不需要组建庞大团队。
businessTips.exampleEn: Instead of offering open-ended "social media consulting" billed hourly, a freelancer creates a fixed $1,500 "30-Day Content Audit" package with a defined deliverable, which she can sell repeatedly, systemize with a checklist, and eventually have an assistant partially execute.
businessTips.exampleZh: 一位自由职业者没有按小时收费提供无边界的「社交媒体咨询」，而是推出固定价格1500美元的「30天内容诊断」套餐，交付内容明确，可以反复销售、用清单标准化流程，日后甚至能让助理部分代为执行。

businessTips.explanationEn: Risk premium is the extra return investors demand for holding a risky asset instead of a safe one like a US Treasury bond — it compensates them for uncertainty about whether they'll actually get their money back. When geopolitical tensions or economic uncertainty rise, investors demand a bigger risk premium, which shows up as higher borrowing costs for risky assets, wider bond spreads, and lower valuations for stocks. Watching how risk premiums move helps investors gauge how nervous the market really is beneath the daily price swings.
businessTips.explanationZh: 风险溢价是投资者为持有风险资产（相对于美国国债等安全资产）而要求的额外回报，用来补偿本金能否收回的不确定性。当地缘政治紧张或经济不确定性上升时，投资者会要求更高的风险溢价，这体现为风险资产借贷成本上升、债券利差扩大、股票估值下降。观察风险溢价的变化，能帮助投资者判断市场在日常价格波动背后究竟有多紧张。
businessTips.exampleEn: This week's Hormuz-driven oil spike is a textbook risk premium event: crude jumped not because global oil supply had actually shrunk yet, but because traders priced in the risk that it might, demanding compensation for that uncertainty before it even materializes.
businessTips.exampleZh: 本周因霍尔木兹海峡局势引发的油价飙升就是典型的风险溢价事件：原油价格上涨并非因为全球供应已经实际减少，而是交易员为「未来可能减少」的风险提前定价，要求获得相应补偿。
